nodes.label: AI as tool for repetitive and simple tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as tool for complex and nuanced tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to use AI for repetitive tasks, like converting data into tables or pulling data from tables

nodes.label: AI as organizational and prioritization aid
nodes.label_negative: AI as source of distraction or inefficiency
nodes.grounding_span: having things grouped by specific priorities makes my day easier

nodes.label: AI as writing assistant for pointers and reminders
nodes.label_negative: AI as full email writer replacing personal voice
nodes.grounding_span: I like to use AI for writing pointers and to make sure I don't forget details, but actually writing the emails is something I prefer to do

nodes.label: AI as delegated task completer
nodes.label_negative: AI as collaborative partner throughout task
nodes.grounding_span: The tasks I use AI for are mostly the simple and repetitive ones that can be delegated completely

nodes.label: AI as meeting note-taking tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as intrusive or distracting in meetings
nodes.grounding_span: keeping track of notes from our meetings as we discuss options and various topics

nodes.label: AI reliability and consistency
nodes.grounding_span: the assistant was certain a word I kept needing to use was misspelled and tried to correct me every time

nodes.label: AI as pattern analysis tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for human judgment
nodes.grounding_span: The pattern analysis portion of my work could eventually be left to AI rather than needing my skills

nodes.label: Company-sanctioned AI tool availability
nodes.grounding_span: my company has a list of specific AI tools and resources we're allowed to use, and right now none of those have the functionality needed for the more complex tasks

nodes.label: Efficiency and ease of work
nodes.grounding_span: having things grouped by specific priorities makes my day easier

nodes.label: Personal authenticity and ownership
nodes.grounding_span: my review findings should come from me

nodes.label: Human judgment and nuanced decision-making
nodes.grounding_span: a lot of my job requires a human's ability to make complex decisions and judgement calls

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for repetitive tasks
nodes.grounding_span: having specific information highlighted for me to focus on is a big help

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's repetitive error
nodes.grounding_span: the assistant was certain a word I kept needing to use was misspelled and tried to correct me every time

nodes.label: Concern about AI replacing pattern analysis skills
nodes.grounding_span: That definitely is something I consider and worry about at times

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as support for human judgment
nodes.grounding_span: I think the most likely outcome is AI becoming a tool to use with those analysis portions of my work, while I get to focus more on the human side of things

nodes.label: Desire for more capable AI tools
nodes.grounding_span: Hopefully, more capable and powerful assistants become available to me

nodes.label: Task segmentation — separates simple repetitive tasks from complex judgment tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to use AI for repetitive tasks... but actually writing the emails is something I prefer to do

nodes.label: Preference for human oversight and final control
nodes.grounding_span: my review findings should come from me
